report_name: HealthNet Insurance Breach Exposes Sensitive Health Data of 11 Million Patients via Misconfigured AWS S3 Bucket

threat_actors.name: Unknown Attackers
threat_actors.description: The unidentified threat actors responsible for the HealthNet Insurance data breach, who exploited a misconfigured AWS S3 bucket to access and exfiltrate sensitive patient data.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Attackers gained initial access by discovering and exploiting a publicly accessible AWS S3 bucket that contained sensitive access keys.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used exposed AWS access keys found in a misconfigured S3 bucket to authenticate to HealthNet's cloud environment.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Attackers accessed and exfiltrated sensitive PII and PHI, including Social Security numbers and medical claims, from cloud databases using compromised credentials.

identities.name: HealthNet Insurance
identities.description: A major US health insurance provider that suffered a data breach affecting 11 million individuals due to a misconfigured AWS S3 bucket.

identities.name: Department of Health and Human Services
identities.description: The US regulatory body, also known as HHS, investigating the HealthNet data breach for potential HIPAA violations.

infrastructure.name: Misconfigured Public AWS S3 Bucket
infrastructure.description: A publicly accessible AWS S3 bucket that contained exposed access keys, which attackers used as a staging point to pivot to sensitive production databases.

infrastructure.name: Attacker Exfiltration Server
infrastructure.description: An external server controlled by the attackers to which sensitive PII and PHI data was transferred from HealthNet's AWS environment.
